# ANALYSIS: ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER_STOP_VALIDATION

**Filed:** 2026-06-21  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** analysis  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

We need to validate whether chart-stop-only exits (SPY close below ORH) outperform the -10% premium stop for ORB long signals across the full period 2025-01 to 2026-05. The ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER watcher already shows strong long-only performance, but the optimal exit setting must be confirmed via real-fills on the full signal set.

## Mechanism

We will run a Stage-1 backtest using the ORB long signal detection logic (breakout above ORH, retest within $0.20 of ORH from above, close above ORH on green bar) for all trading days from 2025-01-01 to 2026-05-31. For each signal, we will simulate two exit rules:
1. Chart-stop-only: exit at close of 3-min bar where SPY closes below ORH (no buffer, or with a small buffer as defined in params.json#chart_stop_buffer_dollars).
2. -10% premium stop: exit when option premium drops to 90% of entry premium (i.e., -10% loss).

We will compute win rate, average P&L per trade, total P&L, and compare the two exit rules. We will also compute OP-16 anchor day performance (if any ORB long signals fire on J's winner/loser days) to ensure no regression.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline. For ORB long signals, typical entry premium ~$0.50-$2.00, so 28 contracts risk ~$1,400-$5,600 per trade at $1.00 entry.
2. **Sample bias:** We will use all ORB long signals from 2025-01 to 2026-05 (approx. 16 months). Selection rule: signals generated by ORB_RETEST_LONG logic (breakout above ORH, retest within $0.20, close above ORH). Overfit risk is mitigated by walk-forward validation and real-fills check on top 3 J days.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (we will perform walk-forward held-out window: train 2025-01-01..2025-09-30, test 2025-10-01..2026-05-31).
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (we will run realistic simulator against OPRA cache for top 3 J days where ORB long signals occur, if any).
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: large adverse gap through ORH causing chart-stop to trigger far below entry.
   - Max drawdown: consecutive losing signals in choppy markets where ORB retests fail.
   - Blow-up scenario: volatility expansion causing ORH to be repeatedly touched and broken, leading to multiple whipsaw entries.
6. **Concentration:** We will disclose concentration after backtest; if top-5 days exceed 50% of P&L, we will flag regime dependence.

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>
- Gym validators for ORB long signal detection must pass.
- Walk-forward OOS/IS Sharpe ratio >= 0.5 for chart-stop-only exit.
- Real-fills validation on top 3 J days (if ORB long signals present) must show chart-stop-only outperforms -10% premium stop by >20% in win rate or P&L.
- No regression on OP-16 anchor days (edge_capture >= 0 for winners, <=0 for losers).

## Confidence

5 / 10 -- We have preliminary real-fills from ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER candidate showing chart-stop-only (N=10 WR=90% P&L=+$273) vs -10% premium stop (N=10 WR=30% FAIL). However, the sample is small (N=10) and may not be representative of the full signal set. We need to run the full backtest to confirm.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

<does this conflict with / complement candidates 1-9 in _LEADERBOARD.md?>
This analysis validates the ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER candidate (ranked #5 in leaderboard) and does not conflict with any existing candidates. It complements ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE (#4) by confirming the exit rule for long-only ORB signals. If chart-stop-only is confirmed optimal, it will strengthen the case for ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER promotion. If -10% premium stop is found superior, we would need to reconsider the exit rule for ORB long signals.
